text.div:
Generated Narrative: ImplementationGuide hl7.fhir.us.davinci-ra

Language: en

RiskAdjustment

The official URL for this implementation guide is: 

http://hl7.org/fhir/us/davinci-ra/ImplementationGuide/hl7.fhir.us.davinci-ra

The Da Vinci Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resource (FHIR) Risk Adjustment Implementation Guide (this IG) describes exchange of risk-based coding gaps among stakeholders such as payers, providers, and government care programs in support of driving towards accurate and complete documentation of health conditions that would lead to more accurate risk-adjustment payment calculations.

name: RiskAdjustment
title: Da Vinci Risk Adjustment Implementation Guide
description: The Da Vinci Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resource (FHIR) Risk Adjustment Implementation Guide (this IG) describes exchange of risk-based coding gaps among stakeholders such as payers, providers, and government care programs in support of driving towards accurate and complete documentation of health conditions that would lead to more accurate risk-adjustment payment calculations.

extension.valueMarkdown: Automatically added as a dependency - all IGs depend on HL7 Terminology
Needed for systems supporting USCDI v4
Needed for systems supporting USCDI v3
Needed for systems supporting USCDI v1
Needed for the RA Data Exchange MeasureReport Profile having an extension using CRMI Software System Device profile

resource.name: Bundle example for the data parameter of the risk-gap operation
resource.description: This is a Bundle example includes referenced resources by parameters of the risk-gap operation.

resource.name: Clinical Data Collection Deadline Extension
resource.description: Indicates the deadline for collecting data to close Condition Category (CC) coding gaps for a risk adjustment model of a measurement period.

resource.name: Coding Gap Remark
resource.description: Coded remark for a coding gap.

resource.name: Coding Gap Remark Codes
resource.description: Code sytstem that indicates coded remark for a coding gap.

resource.name: Condition Category Remark Extension
resource.description: The Condition Category Remark extension allows a coded and/or narrative remark be added to a Condition Category (CC) coding gap that is on a Payer generated risk adjustment coding gap report by a provider, an organization, or a software.

resource.name: Condition Category Type
resource.description: Type of value-based performance reporting performance metric.

resource.name: Condition Category Type Extension
resource.description: Indicates whether the Condition Category (CC) gap is historic or suspected. This extension is also used to indicate if a Condition Category (CC) is net-new.

resource.name: Evaluate Measure
resource.description: The ra.evaluate-measure operation is used to evaluate Digital Condition Category and obtain the results.

resource.name: Evidence Status
resource.description: Codes that indicate whether a coding gap is an open-gap, invalid-gap, closed-gap, or pending.

resource.name: Evidence Status Date Extension
resource.description: Risk adjustment evidence status date

resource.name: Evidence Status Extension
resource.description: Indicates whether a Condition Category (CC) coding gap is closed-gap, open-gap, invalid-gap, or pending based on the clinical evidence available for risk adjustment.

resource.name: Group Reference Extension
resource.description: This extension identifies which Condition Category (CC) the supporting evidence (represented by `MeasureReport.evaluatedResource`) is for.

resource.name: Hierarchical Status
resource.description: Codes that indicate how hierarchies were applied to a Condition Category (CC), and if applied, whether the Condition Category (CC) is superseded.

resource.name: Hierarchical Status Extension
resource.description: Indicates how hiearchies were applied to a Condition Category (CC), and if applied, whether the Condition Category (CC) is superseded.

resource.name: QualifyingDxCode Extension
resource.description: Diagnosis code(s) classified under a Condition Category, as defined by the risk adjustment model. This usually comes into play in the scenario where proprietary risk models are used and the mappings of ICD-10 codes to Condition Categories are not readily available. Note that whether qualifying codes may be included in a report depending may depend on individual Risk Adjustment model licensing agreement.

resource.name: RA Confidence Scale
resource.description: Codes that describe the confidence scale of a risk adjustment Condition Category (CC).

resource.description: This is an example for the Risk Adjustment Model Measure profile. The example uses the CMS-HCC version 21 as the example Risk Adjustment Model.

resource.name: RA Model Example03 CMS-RxHCC version 5
resource.description: This is an example for the Risk Adjustment Model Measure profile. The example uses the CMS-RxHCC version 5 as the example Risk Adjustment Model.

resource.name: RA Parameters ccRemark Patch Profile
resource.description: A Parameters profile that defines structures of multi-part Parameters to be used for sending [Condition Category Remarks](StructureDefinition-ra-ccRemark.html) to a [Risk Adjustment Coding Gap Report](StructureDefinition-ra-measurereport.html) using the [PATCH](https://www.hl7.org/fhir/http.html#patch) operation.

resource.name: RA Temporary Code System
resource.description: Temporary code system defined in this implementation guide

resource.name: Remark Reason
resource.description: Reason for the remark

resource.name: Remark Reason Codes
resource.description: Reason codes for remark

resource.name: Remark Type
resource.description: Type of Condition Category Remark

resource.name: Reporting Vendor Extension
resource.description: A reference to the vendor who generated the report. The 'reporting vendor' is intended to represent the submitting entity when it is not the same as the reporting entity. This extension is used when the Receiver is interested in getting vendor information in the report.

resource.name: Risk Adjustment Coding Gap MeasureReport
resource.description: This profile is based on the [MeasureReport](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/measurereport.html) to represent a risk adjustment coding gap report. Payer runs risk adjustment engine and uses this profile to represent the risk adjustment Condition Category (CC) coding gaps and their detailed information for a patient.

resource.name: Risk Adjustment Coding Gap MeasureReport with Remark
resource.description: This profile represents a risk adjustment coding gap report with condition categories and at least one of the condition category has one or more condition category remark(s).

resource.name: Risk Adjustment Coding Gap Report Bundle
resource.description: The Risk Adjustment Coding Gap Report Bundle Profile describes a collection bundle returned by the [$ra.evaluate-measure](OperationDefinition-ra.evaluate-measure.html) operation. This bundle is a collection of Risk Adjustment Coding Gap Reports.

resource.name: Risk Adjustment Condition Category Type Codes
resource.description: Code sytstem that indicates whether it is a suspected Condition Category (CC) gap, a historic Condition Category (CC) gap, or a net-new Condition Category (CC). Historic, suspected, and net-new are mutually exclusive.

resource.name: Risk Adjustment Data Exchange MeasureReport
resource.description: This profile is based on the [MeasureReport](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/measurereport.html). It is used for Provider to submit data such as clinical evaluation evidence to Payer.

resource.name: Risk Adjustment Data Submission Client Capability Statement
resource.description: This profile defines the expected capabilities of a Da Vinci Risk Adjustment Data Submission Client when conforming to the Da Vinci Risk Adjustment Implementation Guide for interactions between Data Submission Clients and Data Submission Servers. Clients are the actors creating and submitting the Risk Adjustment Data Exchange Measure Reports using the $submit-data operation to the Server.

resource.name: Risk Adjustment Data Submission Server Capability Statement
resource.description: This profile defines the expected capabilities of a Da Vinci Risk Adjustment Data Submission Server when conforming to the Da Vinci Risk Adjustment Implementation Guide for interactions between Clients and Servers. Servers are the actors receiving and processing the Risk Adjustment Data Exchange MeasureReports submitted by the Client.

resource.name: Risk Adjustment Evidence Status Codes
resource.description: Code System that indicates whether a coding gap is an open-gap, invalid-gap, closed-gap or pending

resource.name: Risk Adjustment Hierarchical Status Codes
resource.description: Code system that indicates how hierarchies were applied to a Condition Category (CC), and if applied, whether the Condition Category (CC) is superseded.

resource.name: Risk Adjustment MeasureReport Bundle example, MeasureReport01: Patient01
resource.description: This is an Risk Adjustment MeasureReport Bundle example includes MeasureReport01 for Patient01.

resource.name: Risk Adjustment MeasureReport With Remark Bundle
resource.description: This bundle contains a Risk Adjustment Coding Gap MeasureReport that has Condition Category Remark (ccRemark) present on at least one of the Condition Categories. It also contains the relevant resources referenced by the ccRemark.

resource.name: Risk Adjustment Model Measure
resource.description: This risk adjustment model measure profile is based on the [Measure](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/measure.html) resource to represent a risk adjustment model and its version, such as CMS-HCC version 24, CMS-HCC version 28.

resource.name: Risk Adjustment Reporting Client Capability Statement
resource.description: This profile defines the expected capabilities of a Da Vinci Risk Adjustment Reporting Client when conforming to the Da Vinci Risk Adjustment Implementation Guide for interactions between Reporting Clients and Reporting Servers. Clients are the actors making the request for Risk Adjustment Coding Gap Reports for patient(s) and for Risk Adjustmenet Models that are available on the Server. This CapabilityStatement resource includes the complete list of the *recommended* Da Vinci Risk Adjustment profiles and RESTful operations that a Risk Adjustment Reporting Client could support. Clients have the option of choosing from this list based on their local use cases and other contextual requirements.

resource.name: Risk Adjustment Reporting Server Capability Statement
resource.description: This profile defines the expected capabilities of a Da Vinci Risk Adjustment Reporting Server when conforming to the Da Vinci Risk Adjustment Implementation Guide for interactions between Reporting Clients and Reporting Servers. Servers are the actors receiving the request for Risk Adjustment Coding Gap Reports for patient(s) and for Risk Adjustmenet Models that are available on the Server. This CapabilityStatement resource includes the complete list of the *recommended* Da Vinci Risk Adjustment profiles and RESTful operations that a Risk Adjustment Reporting Server could support. Servers have the option of choosing from this list based on their local use cases and other contextual requirements.

resource.name: Risk Gap
resource.description: The risk-gap operation is used to request condition Category (CC) gaps for a patient/member and coverage within a specified time period, and for a specific context. The context-id is provided as an HTTP header and not as a parameter of the operation. The Client (i.e., Provider) who calls this operation may also POST references to Organization, PracitionerRole, and/or Encounter resources using the parameters. The data parameter is a Bundle, which could be used to provide the referenced resources. Additionally, this operation also includes parameters to povide ccType and evidenceStatus. The Server (i.e. Payer) uses the resources and/or codes provided using the parameters as filter to get the right risk adjustment coding gap reports to return to the Client.

resource.name: Sample CMS Hierarchical Condition Category
resource.description: This is a sample of the concepts that can be found in the CMS Hierarchical Condition Categories (CMS-HCC) code system version 24. The source of the codes included in this example was published at https://www.cms.gov/Medicare/Health-Plans/MedicareAdvtgSpecRateStats/Downloads/Announcement2020.pdf. The CMS-HCC model uses more than 9,000 ICD-10-CM codes, which are mapped to condition categories (CCs) that predict costs well. The condition categories (CCs) are based on diagnoses clinically related to one another and with similar predicted cost implications. Hierarchies are imposed on the Condition Categories (CCs) to capture the most costly diagnoses. Hierarchy logic is imposed on certain Condition Categories (CCs) to account for different hierarchical costs, thus, the term Hierarchical Condition Category, or HCC.

resource.name: Serum Creatinine for Patient 03
resource.description: This is an example for Observation. Serum Creatinine for Patient 03 Nelda Nuclear performed at XYZ Laboratory.

resource.name: Submit Data example for sending a C-CDA document
resource.description: This is an example for using $submit-data oepration to send a C-CDA document as supporting evidence using the RA Data Exchange MeasureReport.

resource.name: Submit Remark Data
resource.description: A [Condition Category Remark](StructureDefinition-ra-ccRemark.html) may reference resources such as Pracitioner and Condition, using Patch to submit the remark may not be feasible. This operation is used to submit a Risk Adjustment Coding Gap Report with one or more ccRemarks on at least one of the Condition Categories, along with the relevant resources referenced by the ccRemark(s).

resource.name: Trypsin [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma for Patient 01
resource.description: Trypsin [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma for Patient 01.

resource.name: Trypsin [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma for Patient 02
resource.description: This is an example for Observation profile. Trypsin [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma for Patient 02 Adam Everyman performed at XYZ Laboratory.
